content:
✔ How Influence Works:
• Players can use their turn to assist or sabotage another’s roll.
• If helping, adds a +1 to their roll.
• If sabotaging, forces a -2 penalty to the target’s roll.

✔ Skill Checks Can Now Be Contested
• If a player attempts to influence another’s roll, the affected player may contest it.
• Contests are resolved via opposed rolling (higher number wins).

✔ Faction-Based Influence Buffs
• Some factions gain automatic influence over certain skill types.
• Example: The Trickster Cult gets +1 when sabotaging opponents.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Allows greater player-to-player interaction.
✔ Introduces risk-reward mechanics to every challenge.
✔ Creates real tactical depth.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 465 (chunk 465/555)
